title: lasthenia californica subsp. bakeri
intro: Lasthenia californica subsp. bakeri, also known as Baker's goldfields, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Lasthenia californica plant, a member of the sunflower family native to California. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. It has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its astringent and soothing effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is often used in traditional folk medicine, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified practitioner.
